task: BUILD-PLAN v3 trinn 4A - global structural contracts (app-shell, header, navigation)
classification_rationale: Touches app-shell, header and main navigation, which are shared by every page. Per cross-cutting rule B such changes default to product-wide, even though the only app-code change is a single test hook.
risk_rationale: The only app-code change is one design-neutral attribute (data-testid) on the single existing <header>. No CSS rule matches [data-testid], no JavaScript reads it, and the DOM tree is otherwise byte-identical. Everything else added is test code and documentation, which cannot reach production. Worst realistic case is a failing test, never a broken app.

evidence_requirements.rationale: No visual or functional change. Reviewer required because the contract is product-wide.

not_in_scope: layout, accessibility, visual and functional gates (steps 5-7)

approved_deviation.requirement_as_stated: nav#tabs must be a direct child of .app
approved_deviation.resolution: Markup was NOT changed. The contract follows the real global-chrome structure: exactly one #tabs-wrap, #tabs-wrap is a direct child of .app, exactly one nav#tabs, nav#tabs is mounted inside #tabs-wrap, and nav#tabs is never inside section[id^="tab-"]. This preserves the intent (navigation is global chrome, never page content) without a structural markup change that would have CSS consequences.
